tests.key: service_worker.js › loads and registers chrome event listeners on import
service_worker.js › central › is a no-op
service_worker.js › getUrlKey › prefixes with url- and strips the fragment
service_worker.js › validTab › accepts http and https tabs
service_worker.js › validTab › rejects empty and internal-scheme tabs
service_worker.js › parseTabId › parses the numeric id from a tabKey
service_worker.js › updateTotals › sums process metrics into processTotals
service_worker.js › updateTotals › defaults missing metrics to 0
service_worker.js › urlUpdates › initializes processes and copies title/favicon/groupId
service_worker.js › urlUpdates › accumulates process stats when a process is supplied
service_worker.js › urlUpdates › uses the url as title when title is missing
service_worker.js › update › persists updates to chrome.storage.local
service_worker.js › getLocalStorage › invokes the callback with the storage result
service_worker.js › getLocalStorage › resolves with the result when no callback is passed
service_worker.js › getTabGroup › resolves null for id -1 or null
service_worker.js › getTabGroup › resolves the group for a real id
service_worker.js › trackGroup › stores the group title by id
service_worker.js › listenToProcesses › registers the processes listener
service_worker.js › listenToProcesses › does not throw when the processes API throws
service_worker.js › closeUrl › reorders allUrls, persists, and invokes the callback
service_worker.js › newUrl › returns early without tabId/url
service_worker.js › newUrl › prepends an unseen url key to allUrls
service_worker.js › updateActiveTabs › queries tabs and persists the rebuilt active list
service_worker.js › processProcesses › bumps samples and persists process totals
service_worker.js › associateProcess › fetches each task tab and merges its url updates
service_worker.js › tabUpdates › resolves {} for an invalid tab
service_worker.js › tabUpdates › resolves keyed url updates for a valid tab
service_worker.js › handleActiveTabsGroupChanges › no-ops when oldValue is missing
service_worker.js › groupTabs › ungroups an unlabeled grouped tab
service_worker.js › groupTabs › ignores pinned tabs
popup.js › truncateString › returns "" for empty/undefined input
popup.js › truncateString › leaves short strings unchanged
popup.js › truncateString › truncates long strings with an ellipsis
popup.js › urlHTML › renders title + favicon and no stats when samples is 0
popup.js › urlHTML › renders an averaged stats block when there are samples
popup.js › urlHTML › truncates long titles via truncateString
popup.js › init › activates an existing TabCommand tab
popup.js › init › creates a pinned tab when none exists
popup.js › loadActiveTabs › renders a div per active tab
deriveGaugeTotals › reads cpu and sums private + js-used memory
deriveGaugeTotals › returns zeros for an empty record
deriveGaugeTotals › tolerates a missing record
deriveGaugeTotals › sums when only one memory field is present
deriveGaugeTotals › reads cpu alone with zero memory
applyDrag › inserts a url dropped from the sidebar into the destination label
applyDrag › moves a url between groups and reports the tab to ungroup
applyDrag › does not report an ungroup when the moved url has no active tab
applyDrag › rewrites positions when a group is reordered
applyDrag › returns null when there is no destination
gaugeFillPercent › returns 100 when value equals base
gaugeFillPercent › returns 0 when value reaches base + max
gaugeFillPercent › returns 50 at the half-way point
gaugeFillPercent › subtracts the base before computing the fraction
gaugeFillPercent › computes the memory arc fraction with a non-zero base
gaugeFillPercent › returns a negative percent when value exceeds base + max
humanReadableNumber › groups thousands with separators
humanReadableNumber › groups millions
humanReadableNumber › parses a numeric string
humanReadableNumber › returns undefined for falsy values
humanReadableNumber › leaves sub-thousand numbers ungrouped
humanReadableNumber › preserves a fractional value
summarizeProcessLoad › returns null when there are no samples
summarizeProcessLoad › averages cpu and memory across samples
summarizeProcessLoad › classifies a heavy tab as excessive
summarizeProcessLoad › classifies a moderate tab as medium
summarizeProcessLoad › classifies a near-idle tab as low
summarizeProcessLoad › derives the bar width from the dominant fraction
LabelCollection › renders the group title and its member urls
LabelCollection › splits members into active and inactive sections
LabelCollection › removeUrl removes the url from the group urlKeys
LabelForm › writes a new label with derived backgroundColor and position
LabelForm › renames an existing label, deleting the old key
LabelFormContainer › shows the Add Group affordance and hides the form when collapsed
LabelFormContainer › expands to reveal the LabelForm when clicked
LabelFormContainer › renders expanded when the expand prop is set
Labels › renders a group for each label
Labels › pins the selected label above the grid
Labels › shows the empty-state CTA when there are no labels
LoadMeter › renders the gauge labels
LoadMeter › reads seeded processTotals on mount without throwing
LoadMeter › handles a processTotals storage change
LoadProcesses › renders no process rows until the API emits
LoadProcesses › renders a row per process when the API emits
LoadProcesses › formats private memory in megabytes
LoadProcesses › removes its listener on unmount without throwing
LoadUrl › renders the title linked to the url
LoadUrl › uses the provided favicon
LoadUrl › falls back to the default favicon when none is provided
LoadUrl › shows the per-tab load readout when processes data is present
LoadUrl › omits the load readout when there is no sampled process data
Tabs › shows the empty-state explainer when there are no active tabs
Tabs › renders a row for each seeded active tab
Tabs › excludes browser-pinned tabs from the active list
Tabs › groups labeled tabs under headings and leaves the rest ungrouped
Tabs › toggles the History section open when its title is clicked
Url › renders the title and favicon for a seeded url
Url › derives the display url from urlKey when there is no title
Url › shows the excessive load indicator for heavy process stats
Url › shows the medium load indicator for moderate process stats
Url › shows the low load indicator for light process stats
Url › hides the load indicator when there are no process samples
Url › renders nothing meaningful without a urlKey
Url › reveals the action row on hover
App › renders the sidebar and the Home page by default
App › renders the page named by uxSettings.page
App › changePage writes uxSettings.page when a sidebar link is clicked
App › navigates when uxSettings changes in storage
Load › renders the active-tab URL list from storage
Load › navigates home when the Home link is clicked
Load › renders with no URL cards when there are no active tabs
Chrome › hydrates labels/uxSettings/autoClosed to {} when absent
Chrome › hydrates activeTabs/allUrls/previousLabels to [] when absent
Chrome › supports a single string key
Chrome › passes through requested keys that are present
Chrome › strips timestamp and drops falsy previousLabels entries
Chrome › set delegates to chrome.storage.local.set
Chrome › remove delegates to chrome.storage.local.remove
chromeShim › hydrates the store from seeded localStorage
chromeShim › hydrates dynamic url-_url_ keys that are not in KNOWN_KEYS
chromeShim › get supports string, array, and all forms
chromeShim › ignores malformed seed values
chromeShim › set mirrors to localStorage and fires onChanged with areaName local
chromeShim › remove clears the key and fires onChanged with the old value
chromeShim › removeListener stops delivering change events
chromeShim › exposes callable no-op action stubs
chromeShim › emits a seeded processes snapshot to onUpdatedWithMemory listeners
chromeShim › does not emit to processes listeners when nothing is seeded
chromeShim › is inert when a real chrome.storage.local is present
chromeShim › shares the known TabCommand storage keys
